title: Rio's Big Feelings Day
synopsis: Rio experiences different emotions throughout her day when her favorite stuffed elephant goes missing, teaching young readers about identifying feelings and finding comfort in family support.

pages.text: Rio woke up happy and stretched her arms wide. The sun was bright and yellow through her window. But when she looked for her favorite stuffed elephant, Peanut, he wasn't there! Rio looked under her pillow and under her blanket. Where was Peanut?

illustration.description: A cheerful bedroom with soft yellow sunlight streaming through a window. Rio, a young Latina girl with curly dark hair in purple pajamas, sits on her bed looking confused. Her bed has rumpled rainbow-colored sheets and pillows scattered around. The room has warm peach walls with colorful toys on shelves.
illustration.alt: Rio sitting on her messy bed looking confused in her sunny bedroom

pages.text: Rio started to feel worried. Her tummy felt funny and her eyes got watery. 'Mama!' she called. 'I can't find Peanut!' Rio felt sad now. She loved Peanut so much. They did everything together - eating breakfast, playing blocks, and taking naps.

illustration.description: Rio standing in her doorway calling for Mama, with tears starting in her big brown eyes. Her face shows clear sadness and worry. The hallway behind her has family photos on sage green walls. Her hands are reaching out toward the viewer, showing her distress through body language.
illustration.alt: Rio looking sad and worried, calling for her mama from her bedroom doorway

pages.text: Mama came quickly and gave Rio a big, warm hug. 'I can see you're feeling sad and scared,' Mama said gently. 'Let's look for Peanut together. Sometimes when we're upset, it helps to have someone we love nearby.' Rio felt a little bit better. Mama always knew just what to say.

illustration.description: Rio's mama, a kind-looking Latina woman with long black hair in a braid, kneeling down to hug Rio. Both have warm expressions of comfort and love. The background shows the cozy hallway with soft lighting. Mama wears a coral-colored cardigan and has gentle, understanding eyes.
illustration.alt: Mama hugging Rio in the hallway, both looking comforted

pages.text: They searched the kitchen, the living room, and even the bathroom. Finally, they found Peanut in the laundry basket, all clean and fluffy! Rio felt so happy and excited. She hugged Peanut tight and laughed. 'I was brave and didn't give up!' Rio said proudly. Mama smiled and said, 'Yes, and it's okay to feel all different feelings. That's what makes you human.'

illustration.description: Rio joyfully hugging her gray stuffed elephant Peanut in the bright laundry room. Her face beams with happiness and relief. Mama stands nearby smiling proudly. The laundry room has cheerful yellow walls, a white basket full of colorful clean clothes, and sunlight coming through a small window.
illustration.alt: Rio happily reunited with her stuffed elephant Peanut in the sunny laundry room

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Rio feel when she couldn't find Peanut?
questions.explanation: Rio felt sad and worried when she couldn't find her favorite stuffed elephant, Peanut.

questions.question: Who helped Rio look for Peanut?
questions.explanation: Rio's mama came to help her look for Peanut and gave her a comforting hug.

questions.question: Where did they find Peanut?
questions.explanation: They found Peanut in the laundry basket, all clean and fluffy.

questions.question: How did Rio feel at the end of the story?
questions.explanation: Rio felt happy when she found Peanut and proud that she was brave and didn't give up.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words
pages.text: Rio woke up ______ and looked for her stuffed elephant. When she couldn't find Peanut, she felt ______ and called for her ______.

pages.title: Complete the Story
pages.text: Mama gave Rio a big ______ and they looked for Peanut together. They found him in the ______ basket, and Rio felt ______ again!
